text: Today we will talk about BioPackathon
It is an event to promote bioinformatics package development
First, let's discuss ensuring reproducibility with Docker
By creating a Dockerfile you can freeze your environment
Next, let's look at conda environment files
You describe all dependencies in environment.yml
Pinning versions is important
You can output current package versions with pip freeze
Save them in requirements.txt
Now let's look at how to use conda in practice
Use conda create to create a new environment
Use conda install to install packages
You can export the environment with conda export
This allows others to reproduce the same environment
Let's move to the next section
Now we will discuss workflow engines
Snakemake is a Python-based workflow engine
You define rules to make data analysis reproducible
Nextflow is another popular workflow management tool
You write pipelines using a DSL
Let's look at an example Snakemake workflow
You explicitly define inputs and outputs
Workflow dependencies are resolved automatically
Moving on to the next topic
In Python, fixing the random seed is important
Set it like np.random.seed(42)
In R, use the set.seed function
In Julia, use Random.seed!
Without fixing the random seed, results may vary
This is especially important in machine learning experiments
Next section
Now let's discuss RNA-seq analysis pipelines
We will also cover single-cell RNA-seq data processing
Seurat and Scanpy are commonly used for scRNA-seq
We will introduce analysis examples using Bioconductor packages
We perform differential expression analysis with DESeq2
edgeR is also a popular alternative
Let's understand the differences between bulk RNA-seq and single-cell
Let's move on to the next topic
Finally, let's summarize the steps for publishing packages on GitHub
Setting up CI and writing tests is also important
Automate your tests with GitHub Actions
We will also explain how to register on PyPI and Bioconductor
Feel free to ask any questions
